finding_results.evidence: Live closing exchange added at chapters/chapter_13.md:169, chapters/chapter_13.md:181, chapters/chapter_13.md:193, and chapters/chapter_13.md:201; Thea asks for privacy, names Ezra's hidden letter and map, blurts that 'the first yes was Ezra's,' and Cora reacts by insisting Dell hear nothing before she does.
finding_results.notes: The closing now contains an on-page leverage shift between Thea and Cora, with Thea visibly withholding until pressure breaks her into a blunt partial confession. Cora's high-contraction, practical register stays distinct from Thea's more measured speech.

summary: Added a live closing confrontation between Thea and Cora so the chapter now tests dialogue under pressure, separates their voices on the page, and makes Ezra's letter an immediate interpersonal wound instead of a document-only reveal.
